title: Benthamia grandis
intro: Benthamia grandis, commonly known as the "orchid tree," is a tropical climbing plant belonging to the family Orchidaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. Bioactive constituents of Benthamia grandis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Benthamia grandis is often used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and powdered extracts, which are used to treat respiratory issues, fever, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Benthamia grandis belongs to the family Orchidaceae and is classified within the subfamily Vanilloideae. This species is further classified into the genus Benthamia, which is a small genus of orchids native to tropical Africa. The exact classification of Benthamia grandis is Benthamia grandis (Rolfe ex Hemsl.) Summerh. This classification indicates that it was initially described by Rolfe ex Hemsl. and later reclassified by Summerh. The classification of Benthamia grandis is still recognized by taxonomists today.
